title: [HWToSMT] Assertion failure on i0 constant
body:
It emits an error but seems like the error is not properly propagated. Also it would be nice if we can avoid unknown loc for error reporting (`<unknown>:0: error: bit-width must be at least 1, but got 0`)
```mlir
$ zero.mlir 
  hw.module @concat(in %lhs : i5, in %rhs : i5, out out : i5) {
    %c0_i0 = hw.constant 0 : i0
    %c = comb.concat %lhs, %c0_i0 : i5, i0
    hw.output %c : i5
  }
  hw.module @concat2(in %lhs: i5, in %rhs: i5, out out: i5) {
    hw.output %lhs : i5
  }

$ circt-lec zero.mlir --c1 concat --c2 concat2 -mlir-print-ir-after-all -verbose-pass-executions^C

[circt-lec] Running "convert-hw-to-smt"
<unknown>:0: error: bit-width must be at least 1, but got 0
circt-lec: /home/uenoku/dev/circt-dev/llvm/mlir/include/mlir/IR/StorageUniquerSupport.h:179: static ConcreteT mlir::detail::StorageUserBase<ConcreteT, BaseT, StorageT, UniquerT, Traits>::get(mlir::MLIRContext*, Args&& ...) [with Args = {llvm::APInt}; ConcreteT = circt::smt::BitVectorAttr; BaseT = mlir::Attribute; StorageT = circt::smt::detail::BitVectorAttrStorage; UniquerT = mlir::detail::AttributeUniquer; Traits = {mlir::TypedAttr::Trait}]: Assertion `succeeded( ConcreteT::verifyInvariants(getDefaultDiagnosticEmitFn(ctx), args...))' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/circt and include the crash backtrace.
Stack dump:
0.      Program arguments: ./build/bin/circt-lec zero.mlir --c1 concat --c2 concat2 -mlir-print-ir-after-all -verbose-pass-executions
 #0 0x00005e7f8081f32f llvm::sys::PrintStackTrace(llvm::raw_ostream&, int) /home/uenoku/dev/circt-dev/llvm/llvm/lib/Support/Unix/Signals.inc:727:3
 #1 0x00005e7f8081cc1f llvm::sys::RunSignalHandlers() /home/uenoku/dev/circt-dev/llvm/llvm/lib/Support/Signals.cpp:105:20
 #2 0x00005e7f8081cf76 SignalHandler(int) /home/uenoku/dev/circt-dev/llvm/llvm/lib/Support/Unix/Signals.inc:413:1
 #3 0x00007ddb87842520 (/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x42520)
 #4 0x00007ddb878969fc __pthread_kill_implementation ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:44:76
```
